Package org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover

Class ProverSequent

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.ProverSequent
All Implemented Interfaces:
IProverSequent, IInternalProverSequent
public final class ProverSequent extends Object implements IInternalProverSequent
This class is the default implementation of the IProverSequent and IInternalProverSequent interfaces.

Prover sequents are implemented to be immutable data structures and take advantage of this to share their instance variables in case they do not change.

In this implementation, the set of all hypotheses is the union of the global, and the local hypotheses. The global hypotheses are shared by all sequents constructed incrementally using this sequent.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProverSequent

      private ProverSequent(ProverSequent seq, ITypeEnvironment typeEnvironment, LinkedHashSet<Predicate> globalHypotheses, LinkedHashSet<Predicate> localHypotheses, LinkedHashSet<Predicate> hiddenHypotheses, LinkedHashSet<Predicate> selectedHypotheses, Predicate goal)
      Constructs a new Prover Sequent incrementally, from an old one, selectively replacing the provided fields, or null in case this field should not be overridden.

      This should always remain a private constructor. All methods using this constructor must ensure that the parameters passed into it have been cloned prior to calling this constructor.

      Parameters:
      seq - The sequent to base the new sequent on. Should never be null.
      typeEnvironment -
      globalHypotheses -
      localHypotheses -
      hiddenHypotheses -
      selectedHypotheses -
      goal -

    • ProverSequent

      public ProverSequent(ITypeEnvironment typeEnv, Collection<Predicate> globalHypSet, Collection<Predicate> hiddenHypSet, Collection<Predicate> selectedHypSet, Predicate goal, Object origin)
      Constructs a new sequent with the given parameters.

      Note :
      The parameters provided to construct the sequent must be consistent in order to construct a proper sequent. In particular:

      • The formulaFactory must be able to parse all predicates of this sequent.
      • All predicates (i.e. all hypotheses and the goal) must be type checked.
      • The type environment provided should contain all free identifiers and carrier sets appearing in the predicates of the sequent and can be used to successfully type check them.
      These checks need to be done before calling this method. The behaviour of the sequent prover is undefined if these checks are not done.

      Parameters:
      typeEnv - The type environment for the sequent. This parameter must not be null. It should be ensured that all predicates can be type checked using this type environment
      globalHypSet - The set of hypotheses, or null iff this set is intended to be empty
      hiddenHypSet - The set of hypotheses to hide
      selectedHypSet - The set of hypotheses to select. The set of hypotheses to select should be contained in the set of initial hypotheses
      goal - The goal. This parameter must not be null

  • Method Details

    • traceCreation

      private void traceCreation()
      Prints the given message to the console in case the debug flag is switched on. Optional given objects are appended to the message, calling their toString() method only if debug mode is enabled.

    • typeEnvironment

      public ISealedTypeEnvironment typeEnvironment()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns the immutable type environment for this sequent.

      This type environment contains all free identifiers (including carrier sets) appearing in the sequent and can be used to successfully type check all predicates appearing in the sequent.

      Specified by:
      typeEnvironment in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      the type environment of the sequent

    • goal

      public Predicate goal()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns the goal predicate of this sequent.
      Specified by:
      goal in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      the goal predicate of this sequent.

    • modify

      public IInternalProverSequent modify(FreeIdentifier[] freshFreeIdents, Collection<Predicate> addhyps, Collection<Predicate> unselAddedHyps, Predicate newGoal)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Method for non-destructive modification of a sequent.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. Returns a new sequent after adding the fresh free identifiers, adding and selecting the given hypotheses, and replacing the goal with the given new goal.

      This operation can only be performed if :

      • All the new free identifiers provided do not appear in the type environment of the sequent, and
      • The hypotheses and new goal provided do not contain predicate variables, and
      • The hypotheses and new goal provided can be successfully type checked using the type environment of the sequent enriched with the new free identifiers.
      These checks are done.

      In case this operation succeeds, but does not modify the sequent, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      modify in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      freshFreeIdents - The fresh identifiers appearing in the given hypotheses and not in the type environment of the sequent, or null in case none.
      addhyps - The hypotheses to add and select, or null in case none.
      unselAddedHyps - the subset of addedhyps to add but not select, or null if none
      newGoal - The new goal to replace with, or null in case no goal replacement is to be done.
      Returns:
      A new sequent with the given free identifiers added to the type environment, the given hypotheses added and selected, and the given goal replaced, or null in case this operation could not be performed.

    • selectHypotheses

      public ProverSequent selectHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> toSelect)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after selecting the given hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The hypotheses that are actually selected are those that are present in the original sequent. Selecting a hidden hypothesis removes it from the hidden set.

      This operation is always successful. In case the sequent is not modified, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      selectHypotheses in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      toSelect - Hypotheses to select, or null in case none.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given hypotheses selected.

    • deselectHypotheses

      public ProverSequent deselectHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> toDeselect)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after deselecting the given hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The hypotheses that are actually deselected are those that are present and selected in the original sequent.

      This operation is always successful. In case the sequent is not modified, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      deselectHypotheses in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      toDeselect - Hypotheses to deselect, or null in case none.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given hypotheses deselected.

    • hideHypotheses

      public ProverSequent hideHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> toHide)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after hiding the given hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The hypotheses that are actually hidden are those that are present in the original sequent. Hiding a selected hypothesis removes it from the selected set.

      This operation is always successful. In case the sequent is not modified, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      hideHypotheses in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      toHide - Hypotheses to hide, or null in case none.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given hypotheses hidden.

    • showHypotheses

      public ProverSequent showHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> toShow)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after showing (i.e. un-hiding) the given hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The hypotheses that are actually shown are those that are present and hidden in the original sequent.

      This operation is always successful. In case the sequent is not modified, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      showHypotheses in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      toShow - Hypotheses to deselect, or null in case none.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given hypotheses shown.

    • isFwdInfApplicable

      private boolean isFwdInfApplicable(TypeChecker checker, Collection<Predicate> hyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedIdents, Collection<Predicate> infHyps)

    • applyFwdInf

      private IInternalProverSequent applyFwdInf(TypeChecker checker, Collection<Predicate> hyps, Collection<Predicate> infHyps)

    • performfwdInf

      public IInternalProverSequent performfwdInf(Collection<Predicate> hyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedIdents, Collection<Predicate> infHyps)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after performing the given forward inference on its hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The forward inference is applicable if:

      • All hyps are present in the hypotheses of the sequent, and
      • All addedIdents do not occur in the type environment of the sequent, and
      • All inferredHyps and hypshypotheses do not contain predicate variables, and
      • All inferredHyps can be successfully type checked using the type environment of the sequent enriched with the addedIdents, and
      • All inferredHyps are not already contained in the hypotheses of the sequent.

      In case the forward inference is applicable, the addedIdents are added to the type environment, the inferredHyps that are not present are added as new hypotheses, and :

      • Selected iff at least one of the hyps is selected
      • Hidden iff all of the hyps are hidden

      This operation is always successful, as soon as it type-checks, and does not contain predicate variables. In case the sequent is not modified, a reference to the original sequent is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==. In case of type-check error, this is returned.

      Specified by:
      performfwdInf in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      hyps - Hypotheses needed to perform the forward inference, or null if none
      addedIdents - Free identifiers added by the forward inference, or null if none
      infHyps - Inferred hypotheses, or null if none.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given forward inference performed on the hypotheses or the sequent itself (i.e. this) in case of type-check error

    • performRewrite

      public IInternalProverSequent performRewrite(Collection<Predicate> hyps, FreeIdentifier[] addedIdents, Collection<Predicate> infHyps, Collection<Predicate> toHide)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalProverSequent
      Returns a new sequent after performing the given rewrite on its hypotheses.

      The original sequent remains unmodified. The rewrite is applicable if and only if the corresponding forward inference is applicable (see IInternalProverSequent.performfwdInf(Collection, FreeIdentifier[], Collection)).

      In case the rewrite is applicable, the corresponding forward inference and hide are performed. Otherwise, this is returned.

      In case the sequent is not modified, this is returned. The case of non-modification can therefore be checked using ==.

      Specified by:
      performRewrite in interface IInternalProverSequent
      Parameters:
      hyps - Hypotheses needed to perform the rewrite, or null if none
      addedIdents - Free identifiers added by the rewrite, or null if none
      infHyps - Inferred hypotheses, or null if none.
      toHide - Hypotheses to hide, must be non empty and all contained in given hyps.
      Returns:
      A sequent with the given rewrite performed on the hypotheses, or the sequent itself (i.e. this) in case not applicable or not modifying the sequent

      public boolean containsHypothesis(Predicate pred)
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hypotheses.

      containsHypothesis in interface IProverSequent
      Parameters:
      pred - The predicate to search for
      Returns:

      public boolean containsHypotheses(Collection<Predicate> preds)
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Tells whether the given predicates belongs to the set of hypotheses.
      Specified by:
      containsHypotheses in interface IProverSequent
      Parameters:
      preds - The predicates to search for
      Returns:
      true iff all the given predicates are hypotheses of this sequent.

    • hypStream

      private Stream<Predicate> hypStream()

    • hypIterable

      public Iterable<Predicate> hypIterable()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns an iterator for all hypotheses of the sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.hypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Specified by:
      hypIterable in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an iterator for all hypotheses occurring in the sequent

    • hiddenHypIterable

      public Iterable<Predicate> hiddenHypIterable()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns an iterator for all hidden hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.hiddenHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Specified by:
      hiddenHypIterable in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an iterator for all hidden hypotheses of this sequent

    • selectedHypIterable

      public Iterable<Predicate> selectedHypIterable()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns an iterator for all selected hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator maintains the order of selected hypotheses. This order is the order in which hypotheses are selected. In case a hypothesis is selected a second time, its order does not get modified.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.selectedHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Specified by:
      selectedHypIterable in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an iterator for all selected hypotheses of this sequent

    • isHidden

      public boolean isHidden(Predicate hyp)
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of hidden hypotheses.
      Specified by:
      isHidden in interface IProverSequent
      Parameters:
      hyp - The predicate to search for.
      Returns:
      true iff the given predicate is a hidden hypothesis of this sequent.

    • isSelected

      public boolean isSelected(Predicate hyp)
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Tells whether the given predicate belongs to the set of selected hypotheses.
      Specified by:
      isSelected in interface IProverSequent
      Parameters:
      hyp - The predicate to search for
      Returns:
      true iff the given predicate is a selected hypothesis of this sequent.

    • visibleHypStream

      private Stream<Predicate> visibleHypStream()

    • visibleHypIterable

      public Iterable<Predicate> visibleHypIterable()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns an iterator for all visible hypotheses of this sequent. The visible hypotheses are all the hypotheses that are not hidden. They may be selected or not.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.visibleHypIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Specified by:
      visibleHypIterable in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an iterator for all visible hypotheses of this sequent

    • visibleMinusSelectedIterable

      public Iterable<Predicate> visibleMinusSelectedIterable()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns an iterator for all visible but not selected (i.e., deselected) hypotheses of this sequent.

      This iterator returns hypotheses in the same order as this set was constructed.

      It is intended to be used in the following way:
      for (Predicate hyp : seq.visibleMinusSelectedIterable())
      { // do something with hyp
      }

      Specified by:
      visibleMinusSelectedIterable in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an iterator for all deselected hypotheses of this sequent

      public FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns the formula factory to use with this sequent.
      Specified by:
      getFormulaFactory in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      the formula factory to use

    • getOrigin

      public Object getOrigin()
      Description copied from interface: IProverSequent
      Returns the origin of this sequent as an object. For instance, within the event-B feature, this method will return a handle to the Proof Obligation (IPOSequent) or Proof (IPRProof) from which this sequent is derived.
      Specified by:
      getOrigin in interface IProverSequent
      Returns:
      an object, or null
